mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling, discount rates and financing costs ease faster than earnings expectations adjust, so S&P 500 prices should rise over the next month. The signal is confirmed if rate cuts coincide with positive 20-day returns and fails if easing does not improve forward equity performance.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are not positive in a majority of periods where FEDFUNDS is declining over the prior 3 months, or if the conditional hit rate is indistinguishable from unconditional returns.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE signals weakening household income and demand, which should depress cyclical earnings expectations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect should strengthen when unemployment rises from already-elevated levels.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered unemployment increases are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if the signal loses directional accuracy after accounting for lagged market trend.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS falls while HOUST also weakens, the combination indicates softer consumer spending and deteriorating housing demand, which should lead to lower equity prices over the next month. The interaction matters because either series alone can be noisy, but joint weakness points to broad demand deterioration.
failure_condition: Reject if joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST are not associated with negative 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the interaction term adds no predictive value beyond the two single-series effects.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in INDPRO indicates rising industrial output and improving supply-side activity, which should support corporate revenue and cyclicals, lifting S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon. The signal fails if production growth is driven by inventory restocking without follow-through in market returns.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month changes in INDPRO do not precede positive 60-day SPX returns at a rate above baseline, or if the rolling 3-year correlation is near zero.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL inflation acceleration coupled with a low FEDFUNDS rate implies negative real rates and delayed policy tightening, which should support equity multiples over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism breaks when inflation is already fully discounted or when the central bank is actively hiking in response.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rises above 4% while FEDFUNDS < 1%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered inflation-and-low-rate episodes do not produce positive 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if returns turn negative once the policy rate starts rising.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth in PAYEMS supports income growth and spending, so sustained monthly gains should translate into positive S&P 500 performance over the next quarter. The signal should fail when payroll gains are concentrated in low-quality rebound months without broader economic confirmation.
failure_condition: Reject if positive monthly PAYEMS changes are not followed by positive 60-day SPX returns more frequently than negative returns, or if the effect disappears after excluding recession recoveries.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 is accelerating while RSAFS is also rising, real output and nominal spending are expanding together, signaling broad demand strength that should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism fails if GDP growth is inventory-driven or retail sales growth is inflation-only without real demand.
failure_condition: Reject if joint acceleration in GDPC1 and RSAFS does not improve 20-day SPX returns relative to baseline, or if the interaction coefficient is not positive in rolling out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle, rising FEDFUNDS should pressure equity valuations with a lag, and the effect is strongest when CPIAUCSL inflation is still elevated because real rates rise faster. This should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month after rate hikes.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises by at least 25 bps over 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if rate-hike episodes are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if high inflation does not strengthen the negative post-hike effect.
